unit: PR2 — Extend sync-shared.ts for the commons→consumer direction

contract.goal: Extend sync-shared.ts to support a NEW sync direction (plugins/commons/cli/lib/ → consumer cli/lib/; plugins/commons/docs/ → consumer docs/) alongside the existing root→plugin direction. ONE planner with source-kind discriminator (substrate-engineer whiteboard finding). Per-flow consumer rules distinguish lib-consumers from doc-consumers. At PR2 commons is content-empty, so the new direction emits zero specs; PR3 starts populating commons. Backward-compat invariant: old root→plugin direction unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: SyncSpec interface gains an `origin: 'root-canonical' | 'commons-canonical'` discriminator field. Existing root→plugin SyncSpecs marked root-canonical; new commons→consumer SyncSpecs marked commons-canonical.
New COMMONS_CONSUMERS = { lib: ['griot', 'guild', 'loom'], docs: ['griot', 'guild', 'loom', 'ev'] } const exported. Per-flow consumer sets distinct (ev needs docs but has no cli/).
planForPlugin extended: for each consumer, walks commons/cli/lib/ (if lib-consumer) and commons/docs/ (if doc-consumer); generates commons-canonical SyncSpecs. commons itself excluded.
DriftRecord gains origin field with matching SyncOrigin type. Drift attribution during the dual-write window is testable.
detectDrift adds conflict-detection guard: any destination claimed by multiple sources emits a 'conflict' DriftRecord and fails --check.
Banner comment rewritten to describe the dual-direction contract in its eventual post-PR3 shape.
Fixture builder rename + split: buildMinimalSourceTree → buildOldDirectionTree; new buildCommonsDirectionTree + buildBothDirectionsTree.
Five new describe blocks: PR2 (a) empty-commons no-op invariant; PR2 (b) populated commons fixture; PR2 (c) conflict-detection guard; commons leaf-source-only invariant; wall-clock budget.
All 635 existing tests pass unchanged + 13 new PR2 tests pass.
node scripts/sync-shared.ts --check passes (no new drift; commons content-empty).
rules_applied: Substrate-engineer whiteboard finding: one planner with source-kind discriminator, not two parallel planners.
Skeptic whiteboard finding: conflict-detection guard as permanent tripwire.
Testing-strategy whiteboard finding: DriftRecord.origin for debug attribution; three-factory fixture split.
Performance whiteboard finding: wall-clock budget assertion catches O(n^2) accidents.
Design-systems whiteboard finding: banner names eventual contract intent, not transitional shape.
Backward-compat invariant: old root→plugin direction unchanged.
Family-shape consistency: SyncSpec.origin and DriftRecord.origin use the same SyncOrigin union.
disqualifiers: Two parallel planners (must be ONE with origin discriminator).
Old root→plugin direction broken or behavior changed.
DriftRecord.origin field absent or inconsistently named with SyncSpec.origin.
COMMONS_CONSUMERS missing the per-flow lib/docs distinction.
Any content moved INTO plugins/commons/ (still PR3+ work).
Wall-clock budget so tight CI flakes.
inputs: cli/marketplace-manifest.test.ts:213-244 (PR1's substrate-first ordering tripwire — confirms commons is in cascade)

actions: Rewrote sync-shared.ts banner comment: describes the dual-direction contract (root-canonical + commons-canonical) in its eventual post-PR3 shape; documents the dual-write tripwire invariant.
Added SyncOrigin type union: 'root-canonical' | 'commons-canonical'.
Added origin field to SyncSpec interface; every existing root→plugin spec marked origin: 'root-canonical'.

Added COMMONS_CONSUMERS const: { lib: ['griot', 'guild', 'loom'], docs: ['griot', 'guild', 'loom', 'ev'] }. Per-flow consumer sets are distinct arrays — even when they happen to overlap, the shape accommodates divergence.

Extended planForPlugin: for each non-commons plugin, additionally walks plugins/commons/cli/lib/ (lib-consumers) and plugins/commons/docs/ (doc-consumers); generates commons-canonical SyncSpecs with destinations under plugins/<consumer>/cli/lib/ or plugins/<consumer>/docs/. Path translation strips the plugins/commons/ prefix and re-roots under each consumer.
Added origin field to DriftRecord (SyncOrigin | null; null for orphans which have no upstream).

Added conflict-detection guard to detectDrift: a map of destination → list of claimants is built from all plans; any destination with >1 claimant emits a kind: 'conflict' DriftRecord naming both sources in the message.

Updated detectDrift's orphan-sweep: 'docs' added to syncManagedDirs for non-commons plugins. commons plugin tree excluded entirely from orphan-sweep (it's the source, not a destination — same logic as .claude-plugin/ and bin/).
Updated applySync's orphan-sweep: same change — docs added for non-commons, commons skipped.
Renamed test fixture buildMinimalSourceTree → buildOldDirectionTree across all callsites.
Added buildCommonsDirectionTree fixture: writes plugins/commons/cli/lib/helpers.ts + plugins/commons/docs/AGENT-CONVENTIONS.md + PANEL-COMPOSITION.md.
Added buildBothDirectionsTree fixture: composes the other two.

Added describe block 'PR2 (a): commons-source planner — empty-commons no-op invariant' (3 tests). Asserts: with buildOldDirectionTree (no commons content), planForPlugin emits zero commons-canonical specs for every lib/doc consumer; applySync drift is empty post-sync; every root-canonical spec carries origin: 'root-canonical'.

Added describe block 'PR2 (b): commons-source planner — populated commons fixture' (5 tests). Asserts: lib-consumers receive commons/cli/lib/* mirror; doc-consumers receive commons/docs/* mirror (ev is doc-only, gets docs but not lib); review-skill + agent-loop-full get zero commons-canonical specs; commons plugin itself never receives; end-to-end byte-equal.

Added describe block 'PR2 (c): conflict-detection guard — dual-write tripwire' (2 tests). Asserts: writing colliding files at plugins/commons/cli/lib/manifest.ts and cli/lib/manifest.ts (both target plugins/griot/cli/lib/manifest.ts) fires a conflict DriftRecord naming both origins in the message; no conflicts when destinations are disjoint.

Added describe block 'PR2: commons is leaf-source-only — substrate invariant' (2 tests). Asserts: COMMONS_CONSUMERS excludes commons from both lib and docs lists; planForPlugin('commons') returns zero specs against fixture trees.
Added describe block 'PR2: wall-clock budget — catches catastrophic O(n^2) regressions' (1 test). Loose 1000ms budget on applySync against buildOldDirectionTree.
Ran npm test → 648/648 passing (635 existing unchanged + 13 new PR2 tests).
Ran node scripts/sync-shared.ts --check → ok (5 plugins synced; commons content-empty means zero new specs, zero new drift).

Ran /guild-validate with evaluator-contract-fit + react-api + test-unit + naming. All approved. One advisory from evaluator-naming about PLUGIN_SHARED_CONSUMERS retaining 'SHARED' working-name vocab — addressed inline: renamed to COMMONS_CONSUMERS (matches the post-PR1 naming convention + drops redundant PLUGIN_ prefix). Re-ran tests after rename: 648/648 still passing.
corrections: Mid-execution: orphan-sweep on the commons plugin tree treated commons's own content as orphans (since commons has zero SyncSpecs in its plan). Test 'PR2 (b) end-to-end byte-equal' failed with ENOENT during applySync — the sweep deleted plugins/commons/cli/lib/helpers.ts before the copy step ran. Fixed by adding `if (plan.plugin === 'commons') continue;` to skip orphan-sweep entirely for commons (same logic as the existing .claude-plugin/ and bin/ exclusions). Applied to both detectDrift and applySync. Re-ran tests: all 648 passing.

Mid-execution: evaluator-naming surfaced advisory about PLUGIN_SHARED_CONSUMERS retaining 'SHARED' from the pre-PR1 working name. Renamed to COMMONS_CONSUMERS for vocabulary consistency. Single replace_all sweep across both files.

changes_since_previous: Built on PR1's commons skeleton (merged to main as PR #18, commit c369504). Branch rebased onto updated main. No new commits between PR1 merge and PR2 start.
notes_for_pr: PR2 ships the planner extension for the commons→consumer sync direction; no content moves into plugins/commons/ at this PR. The new direction is a no-op while commons is content-empty (PR3 starts populating).

The conflict-detection guard is a permanent tripwire — it survives Phase 1's transitional window into Phase 3 and beyond, defending against any future shared/plugin overlap that would silently let one source overwrite another's destination.

Advisory addressed inline (not deferred): renamed PLUGIN_SHARED_CONSUMERS → COMMONS_CONSUMERS per evaluator-naming feedback. The 'SHARED' token was leftover working-name vocab from before the PR1 rename to 'commons'; addressing it here keeps the substrate's vocabulary clean rather than accumulating identifiers that don't match their concept.

Pre-existing advisory still pending (not addressed in PR2): the script filename `scripts/sync-shared.ts` retains the working-name 'shared'. Future cleanup PR — when the dual-direction window stabilizes post-PR3, rename to `scripts/sync-commons.ts` or `scripts/sync-plugins.ts` and update README references.
Backward-compat invariant verified: all 635 existing tests pass UNCHANGED. The 13 new PR2 tests defend the new direction in isolation, the populated case, and the conflict guard.
